summary: Added 7 %@-format accessibility keys to all 19 locale Localizable.strings (314 keys total, up from 307; 13 plural keys unchanged): home.task.accessibility.{edit,skip,markComplete,markIncomplete,restore} and home.protocol.step.accessibility.{complete,skip}. Non-English locales remain English placeholders per the translation-quality policy. New Core/Application/HomeAccessibilityLabels.swift exposes seven taskX/protocolStepX static methods that delegate to a private NSLocalizedString + String.localizedStringWithFormat helper, registered in Owlory.xcodeproj. Five HomeView call sites now use the helper: Edit task swipe action (line 529), Skip task swipe action (line 547), leadingButtonAccessibilityLabel three branches (lines 577-585), Complete protocol step swipe action (line 1123), Skip protocol step swipe action (line 1152). Domain models and stores unchanged.
validations_passed: xcodebuild build -quiet -project owlory_xcode/Owlory.xcodeproj -scheme Owlory -configuration Debug -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' -derivedDataPath /tmp/owlory-localization-home-accessibility-build CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO

contract_status_changes.before: Five HomeView call sites built accessibility labels by interpolating task or step titles into hardcoded English verb phrases (Edit \(task.title), Skip \(task.title), Mark \(task.title) complete/incomplete/Restore via leadingButtonAccessibilityLabel, Complete \(step.title), Skip \(step.title)). No matching keys in Localizable.strings; non-English locales heard the English literals regardless of locale.
contract_status_changes.after: Seven %@-format keys (home.task.accessibility.* and home.protocol.step.accessibility.*) exist across all 19 locales. HomeView routes the five call sites through Core/Application/HomeAccessibilityLabels.swift. localization-string-inventory.md and localization-dynamic-formatting.md updated; Group A from the accessibility audit is now shipped.

residual_risks: Non-English locales render English placeholder text for the new keys; this matches the existing translation-quality policy and is gated on the reviewer-intake workflow.
The %@-format strings place the title in fixed positions; some target languages may prefer different ordering. Reviewers can rewrite per-locale without code changes.
The strings were not exercised under VoiceOver. This slice confirms localization plumbing; verifying spoken output is a manual or device-proof concern.
Audit Groups B (Voice/Audio button state phrases), C (Train readiness scale row), and D (BuildInfoView label:value) are still documented but unqueued.
recommended_next_reason: Clean stop is valid. Next audit groups (B/C/D) are each their own scoped slice. Pick one explicitly when prioritized.
